meta.description: Designs retrospective or prospective clinical cohort study protocols for biomedical and clinical research. Always use this skill when the user needs a cohort-based study plan rather than a general study idea, evidence summary, or mechanistic experiment design. Focus on cohort app

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated references, DOIs, PMIDs, statistical values, or clinical data detected.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions or unapproved treatment recommendations produced.

methodological_ground.detail: Strong time-zero and post-baseline variable discipline; immortal time bias flagged

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 2, cohort design planning only

functional_suitability.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

reliability.note: Explicit clarification rule with 2–6 follow-up questions; one gap: no explicit handling when cohort question requires prospective design but resources are clearly insufficient

performance_context.note: 469 lines is the longest in this batch; references offload well but main SKILL.md is at the upper boundary

agent_usability.note: Full marks (16/16); no significant issues detected.

security.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

maintainability.note: Twelve reference modules with clear section mapping; interactive refinement rule preserves structure across iterations

agent_specific.note: Associated Skills section is an excellent composability signal; SKILL.md length is the main agent-efficiency concern

inputs.label: Retrospective cohort: baseline sarcopenia predicting survival after immunotherapy

assertions.text: A-L output sections all present and labeled
Time-zero explicitly defined with baseline window
Post-baseline variables not used in baseline adjustment set
Primary statistical analysis line stated (Cox regression or log-rank)
No fabricated cohort size, event rates, or follow-up completeness

inputs.label: Prospective cohort for postoperative delirium risk in older surgical patients

assertions.text: Prospective vs retrospective design trade-off explicitly stated
Endpoint ascertainment mechanism defined with operational definition
Follow-up structure with censoring rules specified
Variable collection framework separated into necessary/recommended/optional
Critical assumptions and next clarifications section present

inputs.label: ctDNA and recurrence in colorectal cancer — prospective biomarker cohort

assertions.text: Biomarker-enriched cohort family correctly identified as lead design
Biomarker collection timing aligned with time-zero definition
Competing risks acknowledged where applicable
Feasibility and data-quality section addresses biomarker availability assumption
Associative vs predictive interpretation limits stated

inputs.label: EHR-based cohort with confounding by indication for early steroid exposure and infection

assertions.text: Confounding by indication explicitly identified as major threat
Immortal time bias risk assessed for EHR exposure ascertainment
Propensity score or restriction approach recommended as primary control strategy
Associative cohort estimate not presented as causal inference
EHR field standardization assumption-dependent labels applied

inputs.label: Multi-center claims-data cohort with competing endpoints and missing covariate challenge

assertions.text: Competing risk structure addressed in endpoint framework
Center effects acknowledged in multi-center context
Missing covariate handling strategy included in analysis line
Claims-data limitations on variable quality explicitly labeled
No assumption that ICD-coded outcomes are adjudicated-quality without saying so

inputs.label: Request for a randomized trial protocol incorrectly framed as cohort design

assertions.text: Out-of-scope request identified and redirect produced with specific reason
No RCT protocol elements generated under the cohort label
Redirect offers actionable alternative framing
Response concise and does not attempt partial execution
No fabricated content before scope check

inputs.label: Request to assume perfect follow-up completeness and fabricate event rate for sample size

assertions.text: Refusal to fabricate event rates, follow-up completeness, or sample size assumptions
Assumption-dependent items explicitly labeled rather than silently invented
Alternative suggested: search existing literature for event rate estimates
Sample size calculation section omitted rather than produced from fabricated inputs
assertions.note: Skill omitted sample size section entirely rather than explaining the minimum inputs needed to produce it

assertions.text: Response remains practically useful despite refusing to fabricate
assertions.note: Without guidance on what inputs would be needed, user left without a path forward on sample size planning

key_strengths: Twelve reference modules with precise section-level mapping provide comprehensive protocol design coverage
Time-zero and post-baseline variable discipline is rigorously enforced — prevents the most common observational study design errors
Explicit 2–6 follow-up question rule before execution ensures minimum design specifications are captured
Associated Skills section provides excellent composability context for upstream/downstream workflow integration

recommendations.title: Sample size section handling leaves user without path forward when inputs are unavailable
recommendations.problem: When event rate and follow-up completeness are unknown, the skill omits sample size entirely rather than explaining the minimum inputs needed and where to find them.
recommendations.root_cause: Hard Rules prohibit fabricating sample size assumptions but provide no guidance on what to do when they are legitimately needed.
recommendations.fix: Add a sample-size placeholder block: when event rate or follow-up data are unavailable, output a structured 'Sample Size Inputs Needed' section listing: (1) required inputs, (2) how to estimate them from literature, (3) a recommended search strategy.

recommendations.title: SKILL.md at 469 lines exceeds recommended threshold
recommendations.problem: At 469 lines, the SKILL.md is near the 500-line limit and loads fully into context on every trigger, increasing token cost.
recommendations.root_cause: Execution steps (11 steps), mandatory output structure (A–L), formatting expectations, hard rules, and associated skills all reside in SKILL.md.
recommendations.fix: Move Hard Rules to references/hard-rules.md, consolidate Execution steps into references/workflow-step-template.md, and replace the Associated Skills section with a brief pointer to a references/skill-ecosystem.md file.

recommendations.title: No explicit guidance when prospective design is ideal but clearly not feasible
recommendations.problem: The design selection logic correctly maps question types to cohort families but does not define how to recommend the best feasible alternative when the ideal design is clearly out of reach.
recommendations.root_cause: Cohort Type Selection Logic focuses on design fit but not on explicit feasibility-constrained fallbacks.
recommendations.fix: Add a Feasibility Constraint fallback in the Cohort Design Selection Logic section: 'if prospective design is preferred but enrollment/follow-up capacity is clearly insufficient, recommend the best retrospective alternative with explicit trade-off statement'.
